summary: Kenneth Suna discusses basic investment math for someone starting at age 40, calculating that $11,000/month invested in an S&P 500 Index Fund (10% annual return) with an initial $50,000 would reach $1 million by age 60. The content is general financial planning and investing education, not options trading or premium income strategies.
takeaways: Starting with $50,000 initial capital provides meaningful advantage
S&P 500 Index Funds historically return ~10% annually
$11,000/month investment needed over 20 years to reach $1M from age 40-60
Starting investing later in life requires significantly higher monthly contributions than starting in your 20s-30s
Bulls make money, bears make money, pigs get slaughtered (risk management philosophy)
title: How to start investing at 40 #shorts
